concept.title: Content Preparation & Quality Checks
concept.shortdesc: This section answers questions about the metR's content preparation & quality checks.

othermeta.name: shortdesc
othermeta.content: Answers questions about preparing content and validating quality before document conversion in metR.

section.title: Do the documents need to be cleaned up before conversion?

p.b: Yes. metR requires documents be fully cleaned up before conversion in accordance with the documentation coversion policy.

section.p: metR is designed to process content with varying levels of structure and consistency by applying structure and ontology driven conversion. However, the documents indicate that review, validation, and governance steps may be applied as part of the conversion workflow, and source quality can influence conversion outcomes.

section.title: What common pre-flight check errors can be detected while migrating my document?
section.p: metR can detect issues such as missing headings, inconsistent structure, unsupported formatting, duplicate content, and incomplete metadata. These checks help identify problems early so they can be corrected before conversion, improving accuracy and consistency in the final DITA output.

section.title: What common post-flight check errors can be detected while migrating my document?
section.p: Post-flight checks can identify issues such as incorrect topic structure, broken references, missing or invalid metadata, formatting mismatches, and content that did not map correctly to DITA elements. These checks help ensure the converted content is accurate, consistent, and ready for reuse or publishing.

section.title: Should my images be in a specific format such as .PNG, .JPG, etc. to optimize conversion?

p.b: No. metR can process common image formats such as PNG and JPG without special preparation.

section.p: Using standard, well-supported formats helps ensure images are preserved correctly, but no specific image cleanup or conversion is required before processing.
